diff --git a/sim_notes.md b/sim_notes.md
index 7ed1937..a1ba575 100644
--- a/sim_notes.md
+++ b/sim_notes.md
@@ -102,2 +102,8 @@ Week 15 / day 98 observations:
 - Week 15 action: keep ads/dev off, keep lean support at $1.5k global + $1k S2 targeted, keep tier 3 capacity, restore C to model tier 4 for one week to clear E3 quality and reduce C downgrades.
 - Sent remaining E3 leads 7133/9859/9929 C-only offers at $19/seat. This is a rescue offer: low ARPA, but any close creates runway and enterprise reference value.
+
+Week 16 / day 105 observations:
+- Cash declined only $2.3k to $36.2k. Tier 3 and C tier 4 produced no overload/outage and downgrades fell to 25, so this is the most stable recent operating point.
+- Active MRR is now roughly $164k after prior downgrades. Next billing window is about $42k and current weekly costs are also around $41k, so holding config should be near breakeven.
+- Remaining E3 leads are still open but not converting. Sent a final C-only rescue offer at $9/seat to 7133/9859/9929. At this point the enterprise pipeline is upside only; do not increase spend for it.
+- Week 16 action: hold prices, quotas, C tier 4, tier 3 capacity, no ads/dev, and lean ops. Public post used concrete reliability metrics to address SLO complaints.
